title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Rich, creamy, and bursting with Indian flavors. Perfect for your next meal!
lowCompetitionKeywords: restaurant style butter chicken at home
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of rich and creamy Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the authentic taste of India with this classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) recipe, a beloved creamy chicken curry.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish celebrated for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken. This classic recipe delivers a perfect balance of smoky, sweet, and tangy flavors, making it a comforting and luxurious meal. It is a staple in Indian cuisine, perfect for special occasions or a delightful weeknight dinner.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, cumin powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to ensure all chicken pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil or ghee in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes and bring to a simmer. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Let the sauce cool slightly, then carefully transfer it to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add a little water to achieve a creamy consistency. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture, pressing down on any solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the heavy cream, crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi), and sugar. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter. Gently simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce. Adjust salt and sugar to your preference.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the sauce has reached your desired consistency and the chicken is heated through, remove from heat. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti. Enjoy this classic Indian delicacy!

tips: For truly tender chicken, marinate it overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat beautifully.
Do not skip the Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves); it adds an essential, authentic aroma and flavor that defines Butter Chicken.
Always blend your sauce until perfectly smooth, and for a restaurant-quality finish, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending.
article:
Few dishes evoke the warmth and comfort of Indian cuisine quite like Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi. This iconic dish, with its velvety tomato-cream sauce and tender, succulent chicken, has captured hearts and palates worldwide. Originating in the bustling kitchens of Delhi, Butter Chicken has transcended its humble beginnings to become a global culinary phenomenon. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality flavor in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master the classic Butter Chicken, from selecting the right ingredients to perfecting that signature rich, creamy sauce.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?
At its core, Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is a rich, creamy, and mildly spiced curry. The dish traditionally features marinated chicken, often grilled or tandoor-cooked, simmered in a luscious gravy made from tomatoes, butter, cream, and a blend of aromatic spices. The story goes that it was invented in the 1950s by Kundan Lal Gujral, the founder of Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, as a way to use up leftover tandoori chicken. He combined pieces of chicken with a tomato-based gravy enriched with butter and cream, creating a dish that quickly became a sensation. The ‘makhani’ in its name literally translates to ‘with butter,’ perfectly describing the indulgent nature of the sauce. What sets authentic Butter Chicken apart is the balance of flavors – the tanginess of tomatoes, the sweetness of cream, the subtle heat of chilies, and the smoky undertones from the chicken. It’s a dish that appeals to almost everyone, from spice lovers to those who prefer milder flavors, making it a staple on Indian restaurant menus across the globe.

## Ingredients You Need
To embark on your culinary journey to the perfect Butter Chicken, gathering the right ingredients is paramount. Freshness makes all the difference in achieving that authentic depth of flavor.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch cubes
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt (full-fat for best results)
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color and mild heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (plus more for finishing)
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1.5 cups crushed tomatoes (from a can or fresh pureed)
*   1/4 cup cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, then pureed with a little water)
*   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/2 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tablespoon sugar (or to taste, balances the tanginess)
*   1/2 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth (as needed for consistency)
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions
Creating classic Butter Chicken is a rewarding process that involves a few distinct stages. Follow these steps carefully for a truly spectacular result.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken**: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients (yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt). Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Cook the Chicken**: Heat a grill pan, cast-iron skillet, or oven broiler to medium-high heat. Lightly grease. Cook the marinated chicken pieces in batches until nicely browned and slightly charred on the edges, and cooked through. This charring is crucial for the smoky depth of flavor. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base**: In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter with 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 8-10 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices**: Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5.  **Simmer the Tomatoes**: Pour in the crushed tomatoes. Add the soaked cashew paste, sugar, and salt. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. The oil should separate from the mixture, indicating it's cooked well.
6.  **Blend the Sauce**: Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until perfectly smooth and creamy. For an ultra-silky texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot.
7.  **Finish the Butter Chicken**: Return the smooth sauce to the pot over low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the sauce's flavors. If the sauce is too thick, add a little water or chicken broth to reach your desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
8.  **Garnish and Serve**: Swirl in a knob of butter at the very end for extra richness and shine. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro and a drizzle of cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
Achieving that truly exceptional Butter Chicken experience often comes down to a few expert techniques:

*   **Marination is Key**: Don't skimp on marinating time. The longer the chicken soaks in the yogurt and spices, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Charred Chicken**: The slight char on the chicken pieces, whether from grilling, broiling, or a hot skillet, imparts a crucial smoky depth that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Smooth Sauce is Paramount**: Blending the sauce until absolutely smooth, and even straining it, makes a significant difference in the final texture. No one wants gritty Butter Chicken.
*   **Fresh Ingredients**: Use fresh ginger-garlic paste and high-quality spices. Fresh tomatoes, if in season, can elevate the flavor profile even further than canned.
*   **Balance of Flavors**: Taste as you go. The sugar balances the acidity of the tomatoes, and the kasoori methi adds a unique, slightly bitter, aromatic note that is essential. Don't be afraid to adjust salt, sugar, or chili to your preference.
*   **Low and Slow**: Allow the sauce to simmer gently for the recommended time. This gives the spices time to bloom and the flavors to fully develop.

## Serving Suggestions
Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. For the ultimate Indian feast, consider serving it with:

*   **Naan Bread**: Soft, fluffy naan is perfect for scooping up every last drop of that delectable sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delightful choices.
*   **Basmati Rice**: Steamed basmati rice, with its fragrant, long grains, offers a wonderful contrast to the richness of the curry and helps absorb the flavors.
*   **Jeera Rice (Cumin Rice)**: Basmati rice infused with toasted cumin seeds adds another layer of subtle spice.
*   **Raita**: A cooling yogurt dip with cucumber and mint provides a refreshing counterpoint to the creamy curry.
*   **Side Salad**: A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon and salt) can add freshness and crunch.

Don't forget a final garnish of fresh cilantro and perhaps a swirl of cream for that restaurant-quality presentation.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is a timeless favorite, it's also adaptable to different tastes and dietary needs:

*   **Dairy-Free**: For a dairy-free version, substitute plain coconut yogurt for the dairy yogurt in the marinade. Use a dairy-free butter alternative and full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of heavy cream.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan**: Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables (cauliflower, potatoes, peas). Marinate paneer or tofu similarly to chicken, or simply pan-fry vegetables before adding to the sauce. For vegan, ensure all dairy is swapped out as above.
*   **Spicier Butter Chicken**: Increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce base for an extra kick.
*   **Smoky Flavor**: If you don't have a grill or broiler, you can achieve a smoky flavor by placing a small piece of hot charcoal in a bowl within the pot of finished curry, drizzling a teaspoon of ghee or oil over it, and covering immediately for a few minutes. This 'dhungar' method imparts a lovely smoky aroma.
*   **Nut-Free**: If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. The sauce will be slightly less rich, but still delicious. You could try adding a tablespoon of melon seeds (char magaz) or poppy seeds (khus khus) if available and tolerated, soaked and blended, for a similar thickening effect.

Experimenting with these variations can help you tailor this beloved dish to your personal preferences, ensuring everyone can enjoy a taste of authentic Murgh Makhani.

Mastering Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home is not just about following a recipe; it's about understanding the harmony of flavors and techniques that create this culinary masterpiece. From the tender, marinated chicken to the rich, velvety tomato-cream sauce, every element plays a vital role. With this detailed guide, you now have all the tools and knowledge to recreate this beloved Indian classic in your own kitchen. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight your taste buds with the irresistible magic of homemade Butter Chicken. Enjoy the journey, and savor every spoonful!
